name: Route Peñas Blancas by the Minas de la Zaza — circular from La Zarza
name_original: Ruta Peñas Blancas por la Minas de la Zaza — circular desde La Zarza
region_intro: Jogging routes around La Zarza offer a diverse landscape, characterized by rolling terrain, historical sites, and significant water features. The region features paths that traverse areas near reservoirs and ancient structures, providing varied backdrops for running. Elevation changes are common, with routes often including climbs and descents that challenge runners. Best jogging routes around La Zarza The most popular jogging route is Route Peñas Blancas by the Minas de la Zaza — circular from La Zarza, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging experience with notable elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Alange Castle – Views of the Alange Reservoir loop from La Zarza, a difficult 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This extensive route provides panoramic views of the Alange Reservoir and passes by historical landmarks. Local runners also love the Minas de la Zaza loop from La Zarza, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through areas with historical mining connections, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Jogging around La Zarza is defined by routes near reservoirs, historical sites like Alange Castle, and areas with past mining activity. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present a significant challenge. The routes in La Zarza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 350 runners have used komoot to explore La Zarza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the La Zarza region?
A: The La Zarza area offers nearly 20 dedicated running routes, providing a diverse range of experiences for joggers. These routes are generally characterized by rolling terrain and significant elevation changes, making them suitable for those seeking a challenging run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around La Zarza?
A: Jogging routes in La Zarza feature varied terrain. Depending on the specific location, you might encounter challenging ascents to panoramic viewpoints, such as those near Pico de la Zarza in Fuerteventura, or serene, shaded paths through ancient laurel forests with trickling water and moss-covered rocks, as found in Parque Cultural La Zarza in La Palma. Many routes include climbs and descents, often traversing areas near reservoirs and historical sites.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in La Zarza?
A: Yes, many running routes in La Zarza are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For instance, you can explore the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir – Alange Castle loop from La Zarza&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 14.5 kilometers with notable elevation changes.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the running trails in La Zarza?
A: Most running routes in La Zarza are classified as difficult, with 11 out of 17 routes falling into this category. There are also 6 moderate routes, but no easy options, indicating that the area is best suited for runners looking for a significant challenge with considerable elevation gain and longer distances.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while running in La Zarza?
A: The La Zarza region is rich in both natural beauty and historical significance. Along the routes, you might encounter impressive natural features like the panoramic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt;. In La Palma, Parque Cultural La Zarza offers ancient petroglyphs and dense laurel forests, while in Fuerteventura, Pico de la Zarza provides stunning coastal vistas.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in La Zarza?
A: The running routes in La Zarza are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.75 out of 5 stars. Runners often praise the challenging nature of the trails, the diverse landscapes, and the opportunity to explore historical sites and natural viewpoints.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several running routes in La Zarza feature water elements. Many trails offer views of the Alange Reservoir, providing a scenic backdrop for your run. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Zarza – Minas de la Zaza loop from La Zarza&lt;/a&gt;, which includes significant elevation and passes through areas with historical mining connections near water features.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in La Zarza?
A: The best time to jog in La Zarza depends on the specific location. In Fuerteventura, the 'sea of clouds' phenomenon, particularly frequent in summer, can provide welcome shade and humidity at higher elevations. In La Palma, the dense laurel forests offer a cool and serene environment, making it pleasant even during warmer months. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the historical mining areas around La Zarza?
A: Indeed, you can find running routes that delve into the historical mining landscapes of La Zarza. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas de la Zaza loop from La Zarza&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, taking you through areas with past mining activity over a distance of about 21.6 kilometers.

Q: What makes the La Zarza region unique for running?
A: The La Zarza region offers a unique running experience due to its dual nature across the Canary Islands. In Fuerteventura, it's known for challenging ascents to the island's highest point, Pico de la Zarza, with panoramic ocean views. In La Palma, Parque Cultural La Zarza provides a mystical journey through ancient laurel forests and archaeological sites with petroglyphs. Both locations offer distinct natural beauty and varied terrain, from dry, volcanic landscapes to lush, subtropical woodlands.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a challenging run?
A: Absolutely. La Zarza is well-known for its challenging routes with significant elevation gain. For example, the trails leading to Pico de la Zarza in Fuerteventura involve substantial climbs, rewarding runners with breathtaking panoramic views from the summit. Many of the routes listed in the guide, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282202019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route Peñas Blancas by the Minas de la Zaza — circular from La Zarza&lt;/a&gt;, feature considerable ascents and descents.
tips: La Zarza is a town located on the plain of the Guadiana River, in a geographical area where agricultural areas predominate with the Sierra de Juan Bueno and Peñas Blancas as background walls.

The La Zarza Mines, also known as the Silos de Calañas, are located in the municipality of La Zarza-Perrunal. These mines are part of the Tharsis-La Zarza mining basin and have a rich history dating back to ancient times.
The site is a fascinating testimony to the mining history of the region and offers an insight into ancient and modern mining techniques.
